215	func formattedVersion(version string) string {
216		if version == "dev" {
217			return "v0.0.1-dev"
218		}
219		if !strings.HasPrefix(version, "v") {
220			return "v" + version
221		}
222		return version

261	// checkForUpdates performs automatic update check (synchronous, once per 24h)
262	func (f *CommandFactory) checkForUpdates(cmd *cobra.Command) {
263		// Check if disabled via env var
264		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
265			return
266		}
267	
268		// Load config
269		cfg, err := config.Load()
270		if err != nil {
271			// Silent failure - use defaults
272			cfg = config.DefaultNewConfig()
273		}
274	
275		// Check if disabled via config
276		if cfg.UpdateCheck != nil && !cfg.UpdateCheck.Enabled {
277			return
278		}
279	
280		// Create checker and perform check (synchronous)
281		checker := update.NewChecker(f.Version, cfg)
282		f.updateMu.Lock()
283		f.updateChecker = checker
284		f.updateDone = make(chan struct{})
285		done := f.updateDone
286		f.updateMu.Unlock()
287	
288		// Run the check asynchronously so command startup is never blocked.
289		go func() {
290			defer close(done)
291			_, _ = checker.CheckForUpdate(context.Background())
292			// Also refresh plugin version cache while we have a network call in flight.
293			f.refreshPluginVersionCache()
294		}()
295	}

297	// displayUpdateNotification shows update notification if available
298	func (f *CommandFactory) displayUpdateNotification(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
299		// Skip if disabled via environment variable
300		if os.Getenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK") == "1" {
301			return nil
302		}
303	
304		f.updateMu.Lock()
305		checker := f.updateChecker
306		done := f.updateDone
307		f.updateMu.Unlock()
308	
309		if checker == nil {
310			return nil
311		}
312	
313		if done != nil {
314			select {
315			case <-done:
316			default:
317				return nil
318			}
319		}
320	
321		// Skip update notification for help commands
322		if cmd.Name() == "help" || cmd.Parent() != nil && cmd.Parent().Name() == "help" {
323			return nil
324		}
325	
326		// Skip update notification for machine-readable output formats
327		jsonFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
328		if jsonFlag {
329			return nil
330		}
331	
332		// Skip update notification if silent flag is set
333		silentFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("silent")
334		if silentFlag {
335			return nil
336		}
337	
338		// Skip update notification if --no-check flag is set (for version command)
339		noCheck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-check")
340		if noCheck {
341			return nil
342		}
343	
344		// Skip when stdout is not a terminal (pipes, scripts, CI)
345		if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) {
346			return nil
347		}
348	
349		available, version, err := checker.IsUpdateAvailable()
350		if err != nil || !available {
351			if err != nil {
352				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: Could not check for updates: %v\n", err)
353			}
354			return nil
355		}
356	
357		// Show update notification with changelog for version command
358		// Always write to stderr so it doesn't corrupt JSON pipelines
359		isVersionCmd := cmd.Use == "version"
360		if isVersionCmd {
361			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
362				"\n⬆️  Update available: %s (run 'ddx upgrade' to install)\n\nWhat's new:\n  • Performance improvements\n  • Bug fixes\n  • New features\n",
363				version)
364		} else {
365			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr,
366				"\n⬆️  Update available: %s (run 'ddx upgrade' to install)\n",
367				version)
368		}
369	
370		return nil
371	}

373	// checkVersionGate blocks execution if the binary is older than the project requires.
374	// Returns an error for non-exempt commands when binary version < project's ddx_version.
375	func (f *CommandFactory) checkVersionGate(cmd *cobra.Command) error {
376		// Dev builds bypass the gate
377		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
378			return nil
379		}
380	
381		// Read project versions
382		projectVersion := readProjectVersions(f.WorkingDir)
383		if projectVersion == "" || projectVersion == "dev" {
384			return nil // No versions.yaml or dev project — skip
385		}
386	
387		// Exempt commands that must work even when binary is too old
388		switch cmd.Name() {
389		case "upgrade", "version", "doctor", "init", "help", "completion":
390			return nil
391		}
392	
393		// Compare: is the binary older than what the project requires?
394		needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(f.Version, projectVersion)
395		if err != nil {
396			return nil // Parse error — don't block
397		}
398		if needsUpgrade {
399			return fmt.Errorf("this project requires DDx v%s or newer (you have %s).\nRun 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary",
400				strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),
401				f.Version)
402		}
403	
404		return nil
405	}

407	// displayStalenessHints shows soft hints when project skills or plugins are outdated.
408	func (f *CommandFactory) displayStalenessHints(cmd *cobra.Command) {
409		// Skip for machine-readable output
410		if jsonFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json"); jsonFlag {
411			return
412		}
413		if silentFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("silent"); silentFlag {
414			return
415		}
416		// Skip when stdout is not a terminal (pipes, scripts, CI)
417		if !isatty.IsTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) && !isatty.IsCygwinTerminal(os.Stdout.Fd()) {
418			return
419		}
420	
421		// Dev builds don't hint
422		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
423			return
424		}
425	
426		// Check project staleness
427		projectVersion := readProjectVersions(f.WorkingDir)
428		if projectVersion != "" && projectVersion != "dev" {
429			// Is the binary newer than the project?
430			projectNeedsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(projectVersion, f.Version)
431			if err == nil && projectNeedsUpgrade {
432				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
433					"\n💡 Project skills from DDx v%s (you have %s). Run 'ddx init --force' to update.\n",
434					strings.TrimPrefix(projectVersion, "v"),
435					f.Version)
436			}
437		}
438	
439		// Check plugin staleness (reuse ddx outdated logic)
440		f.displayPluginStalenessHints(cmd)
441	}

443	// displayPluginStalenessHints reads the plugin version cache and shows hints for
444	// any installed plugins that have a newer version available.
445	func (f *CommandFactory) displayPluginStalenessHints(cmd *cobra.Command) {
446		state, err := registry.LoadState()
447		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
448			return
449		}
450	
451		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
452		if err := cache.Load(); err != nil {
453			return // no cache yet — check runs in background, hints appear next time
454		}
455	
456		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
457			latestVersion, ok := cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name]
458			if !ok {
459				continue
460			}
461			needsUpgrade, err := update.NeedsUpgrade(entry.Version, latestVersion)
462			if err == nil && needsUpgrade {
463				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
464					"\n💡 %s %s installed, %s available. Run 'ddx install %s' to update.\n",
465					entry.Name, entry.Version, latestVersion, entry.Name)
466			}
467		}
468	}

470	// refreshPluginVersionCache fetches the latest version for each installed plugin
471	// from GitHub and writes the result to the plugin cache. Called from the
472	// background update goroutine — failures are silent.
473	func (f *CommandFactory) refreshPluginVersionCache() {
474		cache := update.NewPluginCache()
475		_ = cache.Load() // ignore missing-file error
476	
477		if !cache.IsExpired() {
478			return
479		}
480	
481		state, err := registry.LoadState()
482		if err != nil || len(state.Installed) == 0 {
483			return
484		}
485	
486		reg := registry.BuiltinRegistry()
487		updated := false
488		for _, entry := range state.Installed {
489			pkg, err := reg.Find(entry.Name)
490			if err != nil {
491				continue
492			}
493			release, err := update.FetchLatestReleaseForRepo(pkg.Source)
494			if err != nil {
495				continue
496			}
497			latestVersion := strings.TrimPrefix(release.TagName, "v")
498			cache.Data.Plugins[entry.Name] = latestVersion
499			updated = true
500		}
501	
502		if updated {
503			cache.Data.LastCheck = time.Now()
504			_ = cache.Save()
505		}
506	}

609	type sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness struct {
610		ProjectRoot  string
611		BinaryCommit string
612		SourceHead   string
613		AheadCount   string
614	}
615	
616	var ddxBinaryStalenessPathspecs = []string{
617		"Makefile",
618		"install.sh",
619		"cli",
620	}
621	
622	func (s sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness) recoveryCommand() string {
623		return fmt.Sprintf("cd %s && make install", s.ProjectRoot)
624	}
625	
626	func (s sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness) warningMessage() string {
627		return fmt.Sprintf(
628			"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead).\nproject root: %s\nbinary commit: %s\nsource HEAD: %s\ninstalled ddx binary is behind this DDx source checkout.\nrecovery: %s\nRun \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
629			s.BinaryCommit,
630			s.SourceHead,
631			s.AheadCount,
632			s.ProjectRoot,
633			s.BinaryCommit,
634			s.SourceHead,
635			s.recoveryCommand(),
636		)
637	}
638	
639	func (f *CommandFactory) detectInstalledBinaryBehindSource(projectRoot string) *sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness {
640		if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
641			return nil
642		}
643	
644		buildCommit := normalizeVersionCommit(f.Commit)
645		if buildCommit == "" {
646			return nil
647		}
648	
649		repoRoot := strings.TrimSpace(projectRoot)
650		if repoRoot == "" {
651			return nil
652		}
653		if !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
654			repoRoot = gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(repoRoot)
655		}
656		if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
657			return nil
658		}
659	
660		originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
661		if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
662			return nil
663		}
664	
665		buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, buildCommit)
666		if err != nil {
667			return nil
668		}
669		headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
670		if err != nil || buildSHA == headSHA {
671			return nil
672		}
673	
674		if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
675			return nil
676		}
677	
678		aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
679		if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
680			return nil
681		}
682		if !hasDDXBinaryAffectingDiff(repoRoot, buildSHA, headSHA) {
683			return nil
684		}
685	
686		return &sourceCheckoutBinaryStaleness{
687			ProjectRoot:  repoRoot,
688			BinaryCommit: buildSHA,
689			SourceHead:   headSHA,
690			AheadCount:   aheadCount,
691		}
692	}
693	
694	func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
695		repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
696		staleness := f.detectInstalledBinaryBehindSource(repoRoot)
697		if staleness == nil {
698			return
699		}
700		_, _ = fmt.Fprint(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), staleness.warningMessage())
701	}
702	
703	func hasDDXBinaryAffectingDiff(repoRoot, baseSHA, headSHA string) bool {
704		args := []string{"diff", "--name-only", baseSHA + ".." + headSHA, "--"}
705		args = append(args, ddxBinaryStalenessPathspecs...)
706		out, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, args...)
707		return err == nil && strings.TrimSpace(out) != ""
708	}
709	
710	func gitCommandOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
711		out, err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).Output()
712		if err != nil {
713			return "", err
714		}
715		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
716	}
717	
718	func gitResolveCommit(dir, commit string) (string, error) {
719		return gitCommandOutput(dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", commit+"^{commit}")
720	}
721	
722	func isDDXOriginURL(url string) bool {
723		return strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(url), "documentdrivendx/ddx")
724	}
725	
726	// resolveAgentSession looks up a session by ID from the agent session log.
727	// Returns nil if the session is not found or cannot be read.
728	func (f *CommandFactory) resolveAgentSession(sessionID string) *agent.SessionEntry {
729		if sessionID == "" {
730			return nil
731		}
732	
733		cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(f.WorkingDir)
734		if err != nil {
735			return nil
736		}
737	
738		sessionLogDir := ".ddx/agent-logs"
739		if cfg.Agent != nil && cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir != "" {
740			sessionLogDir = cfg.Agent.SessionLogDir
741		}
742	
743		logDir := agent.ResolveLogDir(f.WorkingDir, sessionLogDir)
744		idx, ok, err := agent.FindSessionIndex(logDir, sessionID)
745		if err != nil {
746			return nil
747		}
748		if ok {
749			entry := agent.SessionIndexEntryToLegacy(idx)
750			bodies := agent.LoadSessionBodies(f.WorkingDir, idx)
751			entry.Prompt = bodies.Prompt
752			entry.Response = bodies.Response
753			entry.Stderr = bodies.Stderr
754			return &entry
755		}
756		return nil
757	}

package cmd

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks verifies that ddx init --global creates
// ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into
// ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.
func TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks(t *testing.T) {
	xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
	home := t.TempDir()
	t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
	t.Setenv("HOME", home)

	te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
	_, err := te.RunCommand("init", "--global")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "ddx")

	for _, harness := range []string{".claude", ".agents"} {
		link := filepath.Join(home, harness, "skills", "ddx")
		info, lstatErr := os.Lstat(link)
		require.NoError(t, lstatErr, "%s/skills/ddx should exist", harness)
		require.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "%s/skills/ddx should be a symlink", harness)

		target, readlinkErr := os.Readlink(link)
		require.NoError(t, readlinkErr)
		assert.True(t,
			strings.HasPrefix(filepath.ToSlash(target), filepath.ToSlash(globalPluginDir)),
			"%s/skills/ddx should point into %s, got %s", harness, globalPluginDir, target)
	}
}

// TestInitGlobal_WritesGlobalConfig verifies that ddx init --global writes
// ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.
func TestInitGlobal_WritesGlobalConfig(t *testing.T) {
	xdgDataHome := t.TempDir()
	home := t.TempDir()
	t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDataHome)
	t.Setenv("HOME", home)

	te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
	_, err := te.RunCommand("init", "--global")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	configPath := filepath.Join(xdgDataHome, "ddx", "global", "config.yaml")
	assert.FileExists(t, configPath)

	data, readErr := os.ReadFile(configPath)
	require.NoError(t, readErr)
	content := string(data)
	assert.Contains(t, content, "version:")
	assert.Contains(t, content, "plugins/ddx")
}
